title: ambrosia carduacea
intro: Ambrosia carduacea, also known as the greasewood or bitterweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Ambrosia carduacea include its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold and flu, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Ambrosia carduacea include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water.
